latin: Rex Archimedem vocaverat. 'corona mea aurea est,' dixit, 'an aurifex me fraudavit?' Archimedes diu cogitavit. corona in aquam mersa volumen suum revelavit. fraus detecta est.

questions.prompt: What did the king want to know?
questions.target: Whether his crown was truly gold
choices: Whether his crown was truly gold
How to make a new crown

questions.prompt: How did Archimedes test the crown?
questions.target: By submerging it in water to measure its volume
choices: By submerging it in water to measure its volume
By comparing it to another crown

translation: The king had summoned Archimedes. 'My crown is golden,' he said, 'or has the goldsmith cheated me?' Archimedes thought for a long time. The crown submerged in water revealed its volume. The fraud was detected.
